cataloging_subject.label: Life of Saint Cecilia: six comparative streams
cataloging_subject.scope_note: The edition-level selection represented by University of Michigan CME item CME00145: title-page paratext and printed pages 72-102 of Bertha Ellen Lovewell's 1898 monograph. Its six encoded streams represent four medieval works: three witness versions of one South English Legendary Cecilia life, one Latin Legenda Aurea life, one editorial Harley/Tiberius representation of the expanded Northern Homily Cycle life, and one Older Scots Scottish Legendary life. This record does not collapse those works, their physical witnesses, Lovewell's complete monograph, the U-M encoding, or the repository XML into a single medieval work.

form_statements.description: Three verse works and one Latin prose work represented through five vernacular verse streams and one Latin prose stream, with prose editorial apparatus.
Three rhymed vernacular saints' lives represented through five encoded streams totaling 1,856 XML line elements.
Continuous Latin hagiographic prose encoded in two body paragraphs.

genre_statements.label: Scholarly edition
notes: Edition-level genre describing the cataloged selection, not the medieval source works.

subject_statements.label: Virgin martyrdom, chastity, persecution, and conversion

subject_statements.label: Parallel manuscript versions and textual transmission

summaries.value: Four medieval works about Saint Cecilia's noble Roman birth, vow of chastity, conversion of Valerian and Tiburtius, confrontation with Almachius, martyrdom, and burial are represented through six comparative streams. Three streams are witness versions of one South English Legendary life; the others reproduce the Latin prose Legenda Aurea life, an expanded Northern Homily Cycle life through a Harley/Tiberius editorial tradition, and the Older Scots Scottish Legendary life.

In order: Ashmole 43 South English Legendary text with Laud and Trinity variants; full Latin Legenda Aurea comparison; Bodley 779 South English Legendary version; Cotton Cleopatra D IX South English Legendary version; a Tiberius-labeled Northern version whose complete reading depends on Harley 4196 and Horstmann's H/T edition; and the Cambridge Gg.2.6 Scottish Legendary version.

CME00145 is not the complete 1898 monograph. It encodes the title page and comparative text pages 72-102, omitting Lovewell's preface, historical and grammatical introductions, separate variants and notes, glossaries, bibliography, and index outside that range.

The printed headings are not uniformly reliable manuscript-source labels. 'Bodley 799' is Bodley 779; the Gg.2.6 folio range is corrupt and the current target is ff. 354r-360v; and the complete 464-line 'Tiberius' stream carries Harley folio milestones and directly follows Horstmann's combined H/T edition. The South English, Latin, Northern, and Scots works therefore remain four separately counted works. The three South English witness streams are zero-count representation children of their one abstract work, and the title page is zero-count paratext.

extent.description: Four medieval works are represented in six body streams: one South English Legendary Cecilia life appears in 260-line Ashmole, 257-line Bodley, and 241-line Cleopatra versions; the other works are a Latin prose life, a 464-line Northern life with a short Latin quotation paragraph, and a 634-line Scots life. The XML as a whole has 1,856 L, 340 NOTE, 30 MILESTONE, 63 PB, 10 P (seven in FRONT title matter and three in BODY), and seven HEAD elements. The verse-line total is a digital stream metric and intentionally includes all three South English witness representations; it is not a sum of four abstract-work lengths or normalized critical lineation.

content_parts.label: Title-page paratext
content_parts.summary: The encoded title-page matter for Lovewell's 1898 monograph, principally in Modern English with the embedded Latin motto 'VT CRESCIT', separate from the four medieval works and six comparative body streams.

locators.value: FRONT in DIV1 1; 7 P; 1 HEAD nested in FIGURE

notes: This zero-count paratext part prevents title-page paragraphs from being mistaken for an additional work.

Modern English title matter is representation-level editorial paratext, so eng is intentionally excluded from work-level language statements and discovery facets; the embedded Latin motto is recorded here without changing those boundaries.

content_parts.label: Life of Saint Cecilia in the South English Legendary
content_parts.summary: One anonymous Middle English verse work represented in the edition by three separately encoded witness streams: Ashmole 43, Bodley 779, and Cotton Cleopatra D IX.
notes: No single verse-line extent is assigned to the abstract work because the three encoded versions have distinct representation lengths.

content_parts.label: Ashmole 43 South English Legendary Cecilia
content_parts.summary: Lovewell's 260-line central South English Legendary stream from Ashmole 43, accompanied by dense Laud Misc. 108 and Trinity R.3.25 variant apparatus.
notes: Lovewell says punctuation follows the manuscript pointing as represented by Furnivall; Furnivall's exact target-bearing issued carrier has not yet been found in a complete public scan.

content_parts.label: Legenda Aurea: De sancta Caecilia
content_parts.summary: The full Latin prose Cecilia chapter printed as an independent comparison, not as a direct exemplar assigned to any one vernacular stream.
notes: Lovewell cites Grässe, but the exact copied issue remains unresolved between the 1846 and 1850 editions.

content_parts.label: Bodley 779 South English Legendary Cecilia
content_parts.summary: A 257-line South English Legendary version newly printed in full by Lovewell from an unnamed library-made working transcript.
notes: The printed and XML heading says Bodley 799; the folios and current catalog identify MS Bodley 779.
Lovewell says the otherwise unpointed manuscript was punctuated and capitalized according to modern methods.

content_parts.label: Cotton Cleopatra D IX South English Legendary Cecilia
content_parts.summary: A 241-line South English Legendary version newly printed in full by Lovewell from an unnamed British Museum working transcript.
notes: Lovewell says manuscript pointing was retained; a complete public manuscript facsimile was not found.

content_parts.label: Expanded Northern Homily Cycle Cecilia, Harley/Tiberius editorial text
content_parts.summary: A 464-line Northern Cecilia life headed as Cotton Tiberius E VII but transmitted through Horstmann's combined Harley/Tiberius edition. Tiberius is fire-damaged and incomplete; Harley 4196 preserves the complete stream and supplies the internal folio milestones 192-193.

locators.value: DIV2 5; 464 L; 1 P; 26 NOTE; 2 MILESTONE; 11 PB

notes: This part is not cataloged as a direct complete transcription of Tiberius.
Kölbing's 1877 Harley text on pp. 235-240 is a supporting comparison, while Lovewell says her punctuation follows Horstmann in the main.

content_parts.label: Scottish Legendary: Cecile
content_parts.summary: The 634-line Older Scots Cecilia life from the unique Gg.2.6 witness, printed with punctuation and capitalization based on Horstmann and Metcalfe.
notes: The XML heading's folio range '364a-358b' is corrupt. Internal milestones, the historical catalog, and the next-life boundary support current ff. 354r-360v.
The historical Barbour attribution is obsolete and is not used as accepted authorship.

related_works.title: Life of Saint Cecilia in the South English Legendary
notes: CME00145 contains three edited witness versions of this work in part:ashmole-sel, part:bodley-sel, and part:cleopatra-sel.

related_works.title: De sancta Caecilia in the Legenda Aurea
notes: CME00145 contains the complete Latin prose comparison as part:legenda-aurea. The relation does not assert that it is the direct exemplar of every vernacular version.

related_works.title: Life of Saint Cecilia in the expanded Northern Homily Cycle
notes: CME00145 contains an editorial Harley/Tiberius representation as part:northern-composite.

related_works.title: Cecile in the Scottish Legendary
notes: CME00145 contains the unique-witness Older Scots work as part:scottish-legendary.

evidence.citation: General Corpus CME00145 source XML and structural inspection
evidence.summary: Verifies the work identifier, title matter, language labels, six division order, exact line/paragraph/note/milestone/page-break counts, printed page markers, headings, incipits, and the represented selection boundary.

evidence.citation: Bertha Ellen Lovewell, The Life of St. Cecilia from MS. Ashmole 43 and MS. Cotton Tiberius E. VII (1898), complete Library of Congress scan
evidence.locator: encoded title page = cached PDF page 7; History and Arrangement of the Versions, pp. 31-36; comparative texts, pp. 72-102 = PDF pages 80-110
evidence.summary: Primary evidence for the edition's title, editor, purpose, six-stream arrangement, historical source claims, manuscript descriptions, newly printed versions, anonymous library transcripts, editorial emendation and punctuation practices, and selection pagination.

evidence.citation: Digital Index of Middle English Verse, version 1.0, records 4572 and 2803
evidence.summary: Supports the separation of the South English Legendary and expanded Northern Homily Cycle works, normalized incipits and identifiers, witness censuses, shelfmarks, loci, and the Harley/Tiberius survival distinction.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, MS Ashmole 43 catalog and complete Digital Bodleian object
evidence.summary: Confirms the current shelfmark, first-quarter-fourteenth-century date, complete digital object, and Cecilia locus ff. 185v-188v.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, MS Bodley 779 catalog and complete Digital Bodleian object
evidence.summary: Confirms that Lovewell's printed 'Bodley 799' is current MS Bodley 779, dated to the second quarter of the fifteenth century, with Cecilia at ff. 286r-288v and a complete digital object.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Cotton MS Cleopatra D IX, Saint Cecilia component
evidence.summary: Confirms the current manuscript record and Cecilia locus ff. 155v-158v.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Cotton MS Tiberius E VII, Part II
evidence.summary: Confirms the composite manuscript part, first-half-fifteenth-century date, fire damage, and the broad Northern Homily Cycle component at ff. 101r-281r. DIMEV, rather than the holding record, supplies the exact Cecilia locus ff. 271rb-273va.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Harley MS 4196
evidence.summary: Confirms the late-fourteenth/early-fifteenth-century witness and complete Cecilia locus ff. 191r-193rb.

evidence.citation: Carl Horstmann, Altenglische Legenden, Neue Folge (1881), De sancta Cecilia historia, pp. 159-164
evidence.summary: Provides the combined Harley/Tiberius edited Northern text that underlies Lovewell's complete Tiberius-labeled stream and supports its language, work identity, loci, and editorial route.

evidence.citation: Carl Horstmann, Barbour's des schottischen Nationaldichters Legendensammlung, volume 2 (1882), Cecilia, pp. 164-172
evidence.summary: One of the two printed authorities Lovewell identifies for punctuation and capitalization of the Gg.2.6 Scots stream; its Barbour attribution is historical rather than accepted metadata.

evidence.citation: W. M. Metcalfe, ed., Legends of the Saints in the Scottish Dialect of the Fourteenth Century, volume 2 (1890), Cecilia, pp. 368-386
evidence.summary: Provides Lovewell's second named printed authority for the Scots stream, its witness description, form, language framing, and text.

evidence.citation: A Catalogue of the Manuscripts Preserved in the Library of the University of Cambridge, volume 3 (1858), MS Gg.2.6
evidence.locator: Gg.2.6, item 43 and following item boundary
evidence.summary: Confirms the physical carrier and catalog sequence supporting Cecilia at current ff. 354r-360v, with Lucy beginning f. 361r.

evidence.citation: Jacobus de Voragine, Legenda aurea, ed. Theodor Grässe (1846), chapter CLXIX, pp. 771-776
evidence.summary: One candidate issue for Lovewell's cited Grässe Latin comparison and evidence for its title, authorship, language, prose form, and extent.

evidence.citation: Jacobus de Voragine, Legenda aurea, second edition, ed. Theodor Grässe (1850), chapter CLXIX, pp. 771-776
evidence.summary: The other candidate issue for Lovewell's cited Grässe Latin comparison; retained separately until textual collation resolves the copied issue.

Provides the complete entity, relation, source-route, access, whole-manuscript cache, fixity, rights, editorial-practice, negative-result, and unresolved-question record underlying this descriptive manifest.

open_questions.question: At the individual-reading level, how does Lovewell's complete Tiberius-labeled stream select among Horstmann's Harley and Tiberius readings, Kölbing's Harley text, conjecture, and normalization?
next_steps: Collate Lovewell pp. 92-102 line by line against Horstmann 1881 pp. 159-164 and Kölbing 1877 pp. 235-240.
Obtain complete color facsimiles of Harley 4196 and Cotton Tiberius E VII and mark each reading by witness.

open_questions.question: Who made the Bodley 779 and Cotton Cleopatra D IX working transcripts and Laud/Trinity collations, where do those papers survive, and what changes did Lovewell make?
next_steps: Search Lovewell, Albert S. Cook, Yale Studies in English, Bodleian, British Museum, and Trinity correspondence or archives.
Collate the printed streams against the complete Bodley object and a requested complete Cleopatra facsimile.

open_questions.question: Did Lovewell reproduce the Latin comparison from Grässe's 1846 edition, the 1850 second edition, another issue, or a mixed working source?
next_steps: Collate Lovewell's Latin text, errors, punctuation, and page-break correspondence against both cached complete editions.

open_questions.question: What current specialist sources should govern composition-date ranges for the South English Legendary Cecilia, expanded Northern Homily Cycle Cecilia, and Scottish Legendary Cecile independently of their witness dates?
next_steps: Consult current work-level scholarship for each tradition before adding normalized composition dates.
Continue to distinguish work composition, manuscript production, and the 1898 edition publication.

open_questions.question: Can complete, publication-cleared facsimiles be obtained for Cotton Cleopatra D IX, Harley 4196, Cotton Tiberius E VII, and CUL Gg.2.6?
next_steps: Request each whole manuscript, including binding, covers, edges, flyleaves, blanks, and every leaf, while specifying the target loci separately.
Record quotes, delivery formats, exact coverage, and repository permission for public Git and commercial or noncommercial print-on-demand reuse before redistribution.

notes: This manifest catalogs the six-stream edition selection. The four abstract medieval works and five vernacular representations remain explicit rather than being flattened into one title-author-date record.
The XML's 1,856 line elements, zero line-group elements, and apparatus counts are representation metrics, not normalized critical totals or stanza claims.

Lovewell's italic letters within brackets mark editorially supplied or emended letters. Determining the exact manuscript appearance behind each intervention requires comparison with the facsimiles and source editions recorded in the lineage manifest.
The complete cached manuscript images are source-research assets in the ignored source-cache directory. Their presence does not grant repository redistribution or print-on-demand rights.

Manuscript production dates, repositories, image rights, reproduction request routes, and source-cache fixity remain authoritative in the linked lineage manifest rather than being duplicated wholesale here.
